headline: Qualcomm Drops 21% in 2026 — Is BofA Right to Call It a Sell?
summary: Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) shares are down roughly 21% year-to-date, trading between $134 and $135 intraday on Wednesday. The stock shed another 3.65% on March 10 after a wave of analyst actions, capped by a fresh Underperform call from Bank of America (NYSE:BAC). The question now is whether the sell-side is finally catching up to a story ... Qualcomm Drops 21% in 2026 — Is BofA Right to Call It a Sell?

headline: 1 Value Stock Worth Your Attention and 2 That Underwhelm
summary: The low valuation multiples for value stocks provide a margin of safety that growth stocks rarely offer. However, the challenge lies in determining whether these cheap assets are genuinely undervalued or simply on sale due to their potentially deteriorating business models.

headline: Iran conflict: Can the Fed cut later and cut more?
summary: Investing.com -- The conflict involving Iran and the resulting oil price volatility is reshaping expectations for U.S. monetary policy, with Morgan Stanley arguing that the risk distribution now tilts toward delayed, and potentially deeper, Federal Reserve rate cuts.

headline: Tim Scott hopes Fed Chair Powell investigation 'goes away' to clear Kevin Warsh confirmation
summary: President Donald Trump nominated Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell as Fed chair, but the process has been blocked amid a federal investigation into Powell.

headline: Why The Story Around Williams Companies (WMB) Is Shifting With New Targets And Growth Capex
summary: The fair value estimate for Williams Companies has been updated from US$68.22 to US$76.75, signaling a higher central price target in the current model. This shift lines up with Street research that has been clustering around higher targets, often tied to refreshed growth capex plans and EBITDA expectations through and beyond 2030. As you read on, you will see how this evolving analyst narrative might shape your own view on Williams Companies and what to watch next. Analyst Price Targets...
